faq_categories.name: Bookings & cancellations
faq_categories.description: Questions about reservations, changes, cancellations, refunds, and waitlists for workshops and retreats.

faq_categories.name: Payments & pricing
faq_categories.description: Information about payment methods, discounts, vouchers, and currency.

faq_categories.name: Workshops & festival experience
faq_categories.description: Practical details about what to bring, dress code, photography, and class etiquette.

faq_categories.name: Accessibility & support
faq_categories.description: Accessibility features at venues, support for youth participants, and special accommodation requests.

instructors.name: Akiko Sato
instructors.bio: Akiko Sato is a Tokyo-based butoh artist whose work explores the tension between stillness and eruption. After training in contemporary dance and Noh theater, she turned to butoh as a way to investigate memory, grief, and transformation. Akiko has performed across Japan and Europe and is known for her precise, spacious teaching style that supports both beginners and experienced movers.
instructors.tagline: Slow storms, quiet bodies, unexpected memories.

instructors.name: Ren Takahashi
instructors.bio: Ren Takahashi is a senior butoh practitioner who began dancing in the underground scene of Osaka in the late 1990s. His workshops emphasize the grotesque and the tender living side by side, drawing on ankoku butoh traditions and improvisation scores. Ren mentors younger artists and regularly leads intensives focused on long-form performance and ensemble work.

instructors.name: Mika Kobayashi
instructors.bio: Mika works at the intersection of butoh, somatics, and trauma-informed practice. She facilitates gentle entry points for newcomers and youth, with a focus on consent, choice, and curiosity. Mika leads community projects in Tokyo and Yokohama, often collaborating with live musicians and visual artists.
instructors.tagline: Listening to the body beneath the body.

instructors.name: Daichi Mori
instructors.bio: With a background in physical theater and martial arts, Daichi approaches butoh as a laboratory for weight, gravity, and resistance. His intermediate and advanced workshops often include partner work, floorwork, and stamina training to support larger-scale performance projects.
instructors.tagline: Falling, flying, and everything in between.

instructors.name: Hana Yamamoto
instructors.bio: Hana is a choreographer and educator focused on inclusive dance spaces. She designs workshops that are accessible to different bodies, ages, and movement histories, with an emphasis on imagery, voice, and group scores. Hana also facilitates youth labs and parent–teen classes.
instructors.tagline: Everyone has a dance, even the shy parts.

instructors.name: Kazuo Nishida
instructors.bio: Kazuo is a visiting instructor from Berlin whose work with soundscapes and minimal gesture invites participants into deep states of attention. He often collaborates with experimental musicians and visual projection artists, guiding dancers to respond to shifting environments.
instructors.tagline: The smallest gesture can open the largest space.

instructors.name: Sora Ikeda
instructors.bio: Sora facilitates morning practices that blend meditation, walking scores, and butoh technique. Her classes are designed to be a gentle daily reset and are popular with participants balancing dance with other work.
instructors.tagline: Dawn as a rehearsal for becoming.

instructors.name: Yuri Nakamura
instructors.bio: Yuri works with festival ensembles and long-form intensives. She is particularly interested in durational performance and site-responsive scores, often using outdoor and non-traditional spaces for practice and showing.
instructors.tagline: Staying with the dance until it changes you.

instructors.name: Lena Morita
instructors.bio: Lena facilitates introductory butoh series for adults who are new to movement practice. Her teaching is clear, structured, and playful, breaking down butoh principles into accessible experiments. She also assists in youth and teen programs.

instructors.name: Masato Endo
instructors.bio: Masato specializes in youth and teen butoh labs, creating supportive structures for creative risk-taking. His Sunday series for ages 10–15 introduces butoh imagery through games, storytelling, and collaborative scores.

policies.name: Cancellation & Refund Policy
policies.title: Cancellation and Refund Policy for Workshops, Festivals, and Retreats
policies.content_html:
Cancellation and Refund Policy

This policy applies to all single-day workshops, multi-day intensives, retreats, and festival events organised by the school unless a specific event states otherwise.

1. Participant cancellations

  • More than 14 days before the start time: you may cancel for a full refund, minus a small processing fee (usually 5%).
  • Between 14 and 4 days before the start time: you may cancel for a 50% refund or transfer 100% of your fee as credit to another workshop within 12 months (subject to availability).
  • Within 3 days (72 hours) of the start time: cancellations are not normally refundable. In exceptional circumstances (such as illness or family emergency), please contact us; partial credit may be offered at our discretion.
  • No-shows without prior written notice are not eligible for any refund or credit.

2. Transfers and substitutions

  • You may request to transfer your booking to another date or event up to 4 days before the start time, subject to availability.
  • You may send another person in your place at any time before the workshop begins, provided you notify us in writing and the replacement meets any age or level requirements.

3. Cancellations by the organiser

  • If we need to cancel an event (for example due to instructor illness, low enrollment, or force majeure), you will be offered the choice of a full refund or credit towards a future event.
  • We aim to notify participants of cancellations at least 3 days before the scheduled start whenever possible.

4. Late arrival and partial attendance

  • Refunds are not provided for late arrivals, early departures, or partial attendance.

5. How to request a cancellation

To request a cancellation or transfer, please email us using the contact form on the Help / FAQ page or reply directly to your booking confirmation email. Your request is considered received when we send you written confirmation.

For a printer-friendly version of this policy, use the “Print-friendly view” link on the FAQ page, or open policy_print.html in your browser.

policies.name: Privacy Policy
policies.title: Privacy Policy
policies.content_html:
Privacy Policy

We collect only the personal information necessary to manage your bookings, newsletter subscriptions, and communication with our team. We never sell your data and we store it securely in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

You can update your email preferences or unsubscribe from the newsletter at any time by using the links provided in our emails or contacting us directly.

policies.name: Terms of Service
policies.title: Terms of Service
policies.content_html:
Terms of Service

By booking a workshop, festival event, or retreat through our website, you agree to abide by the studio guidelines, safety instructions, and payment and cancellation terms published on this site.

Participation is at your own risk. If you have any medical conditions or injuries, please consult a health professional before attending and inform the instructor before the session begins.

policies.name: Community Code of Conduct
policies.title: Community Code of Conduct
policies.content_html:
Community Code of Conduct

We are committed to maintaining a learning environment that is respectful, inclusive, and free from harassment. All participants, instructors, and staff are expected to treat one another with care and to respect personal boundaries and consent.

If you experience or witness behaviour that feels unsafe or inappropriate, please speak with an organiser or contact us confidentially via the Help / FAQ page.

venues.name: Tokyo Butoh Main Studio
venues.directions: 5 minutes on foot from Higashi-Shinjuku Station (Exit A2). The entrance is down a short ramp beside the cafe; take the elevator to B1.
venues.notes: Please remove outdoor shoes at the studio entrance. Wheelchair users can access the studio via the side ramp and elevator.

venues.name: Tokyo Annex Studio
venues.directions: 8 minutes on foot from Shin-Okubo Station. The studio is on the 3rd floor of the brick building opposite the supermarket; stairs only.
venues.notes: No elevator in the building. Changing rooms are gendered and relatively small.

venues.name: Kyoto Black Box Theater
venues.directions: Located between Shijo-Kawaramachi and Sanjo stations. Use the main glass entrance; staff can assist with the platform lift for the 3-step threshold.
venues.notes: Audience seating is on movable risers. One accessible restroom is available near the lobby.

venues.name: Online Zoom Studio
venues.directions: Participants receive a Zoom link by email 24 hours before the session. Please ensure a quiet space with enough room to move safely.
venues.notes: Sessions may be recorded for internal reference with consent. Cameras can remain off if you prefer, unless otherwise stated by the instructor.

venues.name: Yokohama Riverside Park
venues.directions: Gather near the large sculpture facing the bay, 7 minutes from Minatomirai Station. Look for the butoh banner or white flag.
venues.notes: Workshops proceed in light rain; in case of heavy rain or storms, the session may move under a covered walkway or be rescheduled.

venues.name: Nagano Mountain Retreat Center
venues.directions: Shuttle buses depart from Kiso-Fukushima Station (JR Chuo Main Line). The ride to the retreat center takes approximately 25 minutes.
venues.notes: Single and shared tatami rooms available. Most indoor spaces are step-free; some forest paths are uneven. Vegetarian and vegan meals can be provided with advance notice.

faq_items.question: What is your cancellation and refund policy?
faq_items.answer_html:
Our standard cancellation and refund policy applies to most workshops, festivals, and retreats.

  • More than 14 days before the start time: full refund minus a small processing fee.
  • Between 14 and 4 days before the start time: 50% refund, or you may transfer 100% of your fee as credit to another event within 12 months (subject to availability).
  • Within 3 days (72 hours) of the start time: cancellations are normally non-refundable. In exceptional cases (such as illness or family emergency), contact us and we may offer partial credit at our discretion.
  • No-shows without prior notice are not eligible for any refund or credit.

For full details, please refer to our Help / FAQ page and the complete policy document.

Print-friendly view of the Cancellation & Refund Policy.

faq_items.question: Can I cancel my workshop if it starts in less than 3 days?
faq_items.answer_html:
If your workshop begins in less than 3 days (72 hours), it falls into our late-cancellation window.

  • Fees are generally non-refundable at this stage.
  • In some cases (such as sudden illness), we may offer partial credit toward a future workshop at our discretion.

Please reply to your booking confirmation email or use the contact form on the Help / FAQ page as soon as possible so we can review your situation.

faq_items.question: How do I cancel or change my booking?
faq_items.answer_html:
To cancel or change a booking, please follow these steps:

  1. Locate your confirmation email and click the “Manage booking” link, or go to the Help / FAQ page and open the contact form.
  2. Include the workshop or retreat title, date, and the name used for the booking.
  3. Tell us whether you wish to cancel, transfer to another date, or send a substitute participant.

Your cancellation is effective once you receive written confirmation from us. Refunds and credits are processed in line with our Cancellation & Refund Policy.

faq_items.question: Can I pay for a workshop at the door?
faq_items.answer_html:
Yes, some workshops offer a “Pay at the door” option. When you open a workshop detail page, look under the Payment options section. If “Pay at the door” is listed, you can reserve your spot online and pay in cash or by card when you arrive.

Please note that for popular events and intensives, only online pre-payment may be allowed to secure your place.

faq_items.question: Do you offer discounts for youth or students?
faq_items.answer_html:
Yes. Many youth and teen workshops are already priced lower, and some adult workshops offer a limited number of discounted spots for students or unwaged participants.

Look for a note in the workshop description or a “Concession” price beside the standard price. If you are unsure, please contact us with the title and date of the workshop you are interested in.

faq_items.question: Can parents or guardians observe youth workshops?
faq_items.answer_html:
In most cases, parents and guardians are welcome to wait in the lobby or common area, but we limit observation inside the studio to protect participants’ focus and privacy.

For youth workshops (ages 10–15), we can sometimes accommodate a quiet observer for part of the session, especially for a participant’s first time. Please use the “Ask a question about this workshop” link on the workshop page and mention the specific date and time so we can confirm what is possible for that session.

faq_items.question: Are your venues wheelchair accessible?
faq_items.answer_html:
Many of our partner venues offer step-free or ramp access, and some also have gender-neutral changing rooms.

  • Tokyo Butoh Main Studio: wheelchair-accessible entrance via ramp and elevator; gender-neutral changing room available.
  • Tokyo Annex Studio: stairs only, no elevator; not wheelchair accessible.
  • Nagano Mountain Retreat Center: most indoor spaces are step-free; some outdoor paths are uneven.

On the Workshops page you can use the Venue features filter (e.g., “Wheelchair accessible entrance”, “Gender-neutral changing room”) to find suitable options.

faq_items.question: What should I wear and bring to a butoh workshop?
faq_items.answer_html:
We recommend comfortable clothing you can move in, such as soft pants and a t-shirt or layers. Many participants prefer light-coloured clothing, but this is not required unless specified.

  • Workshops are usually done barefoot or in soft socks.
  • Bring a water bottle and, for longer sessions, a light snack.
  • For outdoor workshops, bring weather-appropriate layers and something to sit on.

faq_items.question: How often will I receive the newsletter?
faq_items.answer_html:
When you subscribe on the Newsletter page, you can choose your preferred frequency:

  • Monthly digest – one email per month with highlights and upcoming events.
  • Weekly updates – shorter, more frequent reminders.
  • Event reminders – occasional emails just before major events.

You can change these settings or unsubscribe at any time using the links in the footer of each newsletter.

retreats.title: Autumn Bones: Mountain Butoh Intensive
retreats.description: A 5-day residential butoh intensive in the Kiso Valley. Daily practice weaves together sunrise movement, silent walks, and evening ensemble labs, with time for rest and reflection in the mountains.

retreats.title: Silent River: 3-Day Butoh Residency
retreats.description: A quieter, small-group retreat focusing on slow, contemplative butoh practice, writing, and time by the river. Ideal for those who want depth without a full-week commitment.

retreats.title: City Shadows: Weekend Intensive in Tokyo
retreats.description: A non-residential weekend intensive exploring urban butoh scores in the heart of Tokyo. Long-form group sessions with structured feedback.

retreats.title: Winter Skin: Online Butoh Lab
retreats.description: A 4-evening online intensive connecting participants across time zones. Each session builds a personal practice around stillness, breath, and image.

workshops.title: First Ashes: Beginner Butoh Evening Intro
workshops.subtitle: Gentle entry into butoh imagery and slow time
workshops.description: A welcoming introduction to butoh for absolute beginners. We will work with simple walking scores, slow-motion improvisation, and image-based explorations suitable for any body.
workshops.summary_line: Beginner • 2.5 hours • Evening • Tokyo Butoh Main Studio

workshops.title: Beginner Butoh: Evening of Slow Time
workshops.subtitle: Exploring weight, gravity, and stillness
workshops.description: This session introduces basic butoh principles through guided imagery, partner mirroring, and quiet floorwork. No prior experience needed.
workshops.summary_line: Beginner • 2 hours • Evening • Tokyo Butoh Main Studio

workshops.title: Soft Bones: Gentle Intro to Butoh
workshops.subtitle: Finding movement in stillness
workshops.description: We will explore softness, breath, and small gestures as an introduction to butoh. Ideal if you are nervous about starting or returning after a long break.
workshops.summary_line: Beginner • 2 hours • Evening • Tokyo Butoh Main Studio

workshops.title: Urban Shadows: Butoh Improvisation Lab
workshops.subtitle: City images, grotesque tenderness
workshops.description: An intermediate lab with Ren Takahashi focusing on improvisation scores drawn from the textures and rhythms of the city. We will move between the grotesque and the tender, working in solos and small groups.
workshops.summary_line: Intermediate • 2.5 hours • Evening • Tokyo Butoh Main Studio

workshops.title: Festival Morning Lab: Gravity Scores
workshops.subtitle: Intermediate morning practice
workshops.description: Part of the Summer Butoh Week 2025. This morning session works with falling, catching, and leaning scores to develop trust and responsiveness in the group.

workshops.title: Festival Afternoon Lab: Texture and Voice
workshops.subtitle: Intermediate ensemble workshop
workshops.description: An afternoon festival workshop exploring vocal textures alongside movement. We will work in trios and quartets to build short ensemble scores.

workshops.title: Festival Evening Lab: Night Rivers
workshops.subtitle: Intermediate evening performance lab
workshops.description: An evening festival workshop focusing on group composition and simple lighting cues. We will create short sequences that may be shared informally at the end of the week.

workshops.title: Sunday Youth Lab: Stories in Slow Motion
workshops.subtitle: Butoh-inspired play for ages 10–15
workshops.description: A playful but focused youth workshop where we turn stories and cartoons into slow-motion movement. Ideal for curious young movers with or without dance experience.

workshops.title: Twilight Butoh for All Bodies
workshops.subtitle: Accessible evening workshop for adults
workshops.description: An open, all-levels class focused on accessibility and choice. Options are offered for dancing seated, standing, or using mobility aids. No prior dance experience is required.
workshops.summary_line: All levels • 2 hours • Evening • Wheelchair accessible, gender-neutral changing room

workshops.title: Long-Form Lab: Staying with the Image
workshops.subtitle: 3-hour intermediate practice
workshops.description: A deep afternoon lab with Yuri Nakamura focused on sustaining a single image over time. We will alternate between solo and group scores and brief reflective writing.

workshops.title: Deep Dive: Bodies of Fog
workshops.subtitle: Extended butoh workshop with live sound
workshops.description: A 3.5-hour intermediate workshop with Akiko Sato and live musician. We will work with long scores, shifting states, and subtle sound cues, building toward a short in-studio sharing.
workshops.summary_line: Intermediate • 3.5 hours • Afternoon • In-studio with live music

workshops.title: Midnight Zoom Butoh: Quiet Rooms
workshops.subtitle: Online late-night practice
workshops.description: An online open-level butoh practice designed for small or shared spaces. We will work mostly in place, with options to keep cameras off or dimmed.
workshops.summary_line: Open level • 1.5 hours • Online evening on Zoom

workshops.title: Family Butoh Walk: Riverside Figures
workshops.subtitle: Outdoor workshop for all ages
workshops.description: A gentle outdoor workshop in Yokohama Riverside Park for families and mixed-age groups. We will create slow-motion walking processions and simple group shapes by the water.
workshops.summary_line: All ages • 2 hours • Outdoor at Yokohama Riverside Park
